label: Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath, SIDE SPLIT — full-depth bedroom column on one side; the other side splits front/back into a great room (front, hosts entry) and a rear service band with the bath and kitchen side-by-side. The T&B sits BETWEEN the bedroom and the kitchen, buffering the bedroom from kitchen noise; its door opens from the kitchen aisle. Sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr (same 6x4 m program, wet rooms in a rear band instead of an end column).

adjacencies.note: bedroom's only door — opens east into the great room across the column seam. 1.2 m floor = 0.9 m clear door + frames.

bath sits directly beside the bedroom in the rear band — shared wall only, no door, per the locked no-bath-through-bedroom rule. The bath is deliberately BETWEEN the bedroom and the kitchen, buffering the bedroom from kitchen noise/smells.

bath's only door — opens east into the kitchen aisle, on the shared plumbing wall (2026-07-16 revision, per client direction): routing the bath through the kitchen frees the great room's entire rear-wall left segment for furniture (the prime TV/console wall) and consolidates all service circulation in one pocket. Accepts the bath_door_into_kitchen sanitary soft warning, same trade-off as the squarish and narrow siblings. NOTE: authored as a FIXED door, not a door_host_group — with a group, Pass 1a's auto-scorer always flips the door back to the great room (the front-door spine's +4.0 circulation-overlap weight dwarfs the freed-wall bonus minus the sanitary penalty), so a group cannot hold the kitchen as the effective default.

solid wall — no door (the bath doors off the kitchen instead, keeping this stretch of the great room's rear wall furnishable). This adjacency also pins the rear band's depth: bath and kitchen must BOTH touch the great room's rear edge, which forces them to equal depth with no extra constraint.

kitchen (rear) and great room (front) STACK — the catalog's default LDK arrangement, no ldk_horizontal needed. Open plan with a dining counter along the horizontal seam, band + 2 stools inside the great room (counter_side) so the kitchen keeps its full work aisle. min_shared_wall_m 2.1 = 2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass; also floors the kitchen's width at 2.1 m.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_side_split_bath_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Ultra-compact wide unit (canonical 6x4 m envelope, 24 sqm), same tier values as the split_wing sibling: bath floors at 2.6 sqm and caps at 4.5 so snap_gaps can't balloon it; bedroom caps at 9.5 so freed area flows to the great room.

notes: Third 1BR entry in the wide catalog; sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr
(same ~24 sqm program on the same canonical 10x8 lot / 6x4 m envelope) with a
different service arrangement: instead of a wet END COLUMN, the bath and
kitchen form a side-by-side REAR BAND above the great room, with the bath
between the bedroom and the kitchen.
Layout intent (front at bottom, canonical form — kitchen on the right per
the catalog's symmetry convention; a left-mirrored brief flips the whole
plan, giving the kitchen|bath|bedroom reading order):
Rear:  [BEDROOM (cont.) | BATH | KITCHEN]
Front: [BEDROOM         | GREAT ROOM    ]
[porch/entry]
Bath access (2026-07-16 revision): the bath door opens from the KITCHEN
side (a fixed door on the plumbing wall) — freeing the great
room's entire rear-wall left segment, its prime furniture wall (the front
wall carries the entry + windows). This accepts the bath_door_into_kitchen
sanitary soft warning, like the squarish and narrow siblings. A door_host
group was tried and abandoned: Pass 1a's auto-scorer always flips the door
back to the great room (front-door-spine circulation overlap, +4.0 weight,
dwarfs freed-wall minus sanitary), so only a fixed door holds this choice.
This is the LEAST override-hungry 1BR topology: kitchen+great stack
front-to-rear (default LDK rule holds), kitchen touches the rear wall
(rear-pin, dirty kitchen, and back door conventions all intact), and the
kitchen sits in the right half (symmetry break satisfied). Only
private_area_floor: false is needed (bedroom ~9 sqm vs great+kitchen
~12 sqm — same 1BR rationale as all siblings).
Geometry note: the rear band's uniform depth needs no explicit constraint —
bath<->great and kitchen<->great must both touch, and with both rear-
anchored that forces bath.depth == kitchen.depth automatically.
Dining is at the counter (counter_divider, counter_side great), on the
HORIZONTAL kitchen/great seam — first counter use on a N/S wall in the
wide catalog. The 2.1 m min_shared_wall doubles as the kitchen's width
floor, leaving the bath ~1.6 m of band width beside it.
No fallback declared. Not a mirror-image variant of split_wing — the
service arrangement (band vs column), bath position (buffering vs front),
and LDK orientation (stacked vs side-by-side) all differ.
